title: $occmod
text: $occmod Return the modification status of an occurrence. $occmod { ( Entity ) } Example: vMod = $occmod (CUSTOMER) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. If omitted, the current entity is used. Return Values Table 2. Values returned by $occmod Value Meaning 1 Occurrence has been removed, or at least one field has been modified. The field can be any database field or a non-database field (if Entity is a non-database entity). 0 Occurrence has not been removed and no field has been modified. " " Entity does not exist or is not included in the component structure. Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Description $occmod is an occurrence-level function that tests the modification status of data in the current occurrence of the specified entity. The $occmod function is set to 0 by the following events: Each time a component is restarted (unless Component Behavior is set to Keep component definition and data in memory ). By the following ProcScript commands: retrieve (Note that $occmod can be set to 1 before a retrieve has been performed.) store release clear reload Events that cause a field to be modified (and $occmod set to 1) include: The user enters a retrieve profile in an empty field. The user changes the value of data that has been retrieved. A non-database field is assigned a new value in ProcScript without the /init switch. Example: Using $occmod In this example, $occmod checks whether the current occurrence of CUSTOMER has been modified, and records this status in the message frame. variables numeric vMod endvariables vMod = $occmod (CUSTOMER) selectcase vMod case 0 putmess "The occurrence is unmodified" case 1 putmess "The occurrence has been modified" endselectcase Copy Related Links $formmod (deprecated) Return the modification status of data in the current Form component. $occcheck Return or set the requirement for checking an occurrence. $occdbmod Return the modification status of a database occurrence.
